hypothesis: Mutating position 5 to aromatic residues (W/F/Y) will enhance SSTR2-specific interactions in the ECL2/TM5 pocket, while mutating position 11 to hydrophobic residues (L/I/V) will reduce off-target SSTR3/SSTR5 binding by avoiding steric clashes in their conserved pockets.

critic_feedback_used.hypothesis: The current hypothesis is that SSTR2 selectivity can be improved by disrupting conserved pocket interactions at non-pharmacophore positions. This hypothesis remains valid, but more targeted mutagenesis is required to achieve a Δmargin significantly greater than native SST-14.

proposed_changes.rationale: To increase SSTR2 selectivity, we must disrupt conserved pocket interactions at non-pharmacophore positions (1, 2, 5, 6, 11, 12) that are likely responsible for off-target binding to SSTR3/SSTR5.
These mutations are designed to break conserved interactions in SSTR3/SSTR5 pockets while maintaining overall peptide stability and pharmacophore integrity.
